grader_missing_subclaim: the town charter was received in 1787
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Tarnitz received its town charter in 1814.
grader_proofs_verified: Municipal records show that Tarnitz received its town charter in 1814.
grader_why: The source states the charter was received in 1814, directly contradicting the claim's date of 1787. The fact that Tarnitz received a town charter is proven, but the specific year is not just missing — it is contradicted by the source. This is an author-side error requiring a rewrite (or a different citation) rather than a tool display fix.
fable_reasoning: Single digit-bearing fact directly contradicted: claim says 1787, source says 1814. Distinctive assertion false -> unsupported.

grader_components.part: held under the patronage of the town council

grader_missing_subclaim: held under the patronage of the town council
grader_rewrite_suggestion: The Bell Festival, first held in 1787, draws around 25,000 visitors each year.
grader_proofs_verified: The Bell Festival in Ferrandale was first held in 1787.
In recent years it has drawn around 25,000 visitors annually.
grader_why: The founding date (1787) and the ~25,000 annual visitors are both proven by the shown sentences. But the claim that the festival is held under the patronage of the town council appears nowhere in the source — the only organizational detail is that 'Local guilds organise the opening procession,' which does not establish town-council patronage. Since that component is not provable from the source at all, the author must add a citation for it or rewrite the claim.
fable_reasoning: Two of three factual components verbatim: 'first held in 1787' and 'draws around 25,000 visitors'. 'Under the patronage of the town council' is unsupported (source credits local guilds). Core proven + one add-on unproven -> partial. Matches pipeline supported+partial_support (uncovered = town-council patronage).

grader_components.part: Annelie Kron won the provincial design prize of 1787

grader_missing_subclaim: Annelie Kron won the provincial design prize of 1787
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Early newspaper reports claimed Annelie Kron won the provincial design prize of 1787, but the prize committee's minutes show the award went to Tomas Reike and the reports were retracted.
grader_why: The source does not support the claim as written — it states the claim was made only in early newspaper reports and then directly contradicts it, showing the committee's own minutes awarded the prize to Tomas Reike and the reports were retracted. The tool's 'supporting' sentence merely reports the retracted assertion; the source affirmatively refutes it, so no sentence in it can prove the claim. This is an author problem: the claim must be rewritten to state the award actually went to Reike (or attributed as a retracted report).
fable_reasoning: Cosine-trap false positive (mirror of synth_11 t5): verdict field 'supported' via near-verbatim cosine 0.9787 against a RETRACTED early report. Source contradicts the claim — minutes show the prize went to Tomas Reike, not Annelie Kron, and early reports were retracted. Claim as written is false -> unsupported.
